findings.suggestion: Use a shared reentrancy guard across functions that touch this key

findings.message: Function 'add_liquidity' performs unchecked 'reserve_a + amount_a' on a financial type — possible overflow/underflow
findings.suggestion: Use reserve_a.checked_add(amount_a) instead of the bare operator

findings.message: Function 'add_liquidity' performs unchecked 'reserve_b + amount_b' on a financial type — possible overflow/underflow
findings.suggestion: Use reserve_b.checked_add(amount_b) instead of the bare operator

findings.message: Function 'swap' divides by unchecked divisor '(reserve_a+amount_in)' — possible division by zero
findings.suggestion: Check '(reserve_a+amount_in)' for zero before dividing, or use checked_div

findings.message: Function 'swap' performs unchecked 'reserve_b * amount_in' on a financial type — possible overflow/underflow
findings.suggestion: Use reserve_b.checked_mul(amount_in) instead of the bare operator

findings.message: Function 'swap' performs unchecked 'reserve_a + amount_in' on a financial type — possible overflow/underflow
findings.suggestion: Use reserve_a.checked_add(amount_in) instead of the bare operator

findings.suggestion: Use reserve_a.checked_add(amount_in) instead of the bare operator

findings.message: Function 'add_liquidity' modifies state without authorization check
findings.suggestion: Add require_auth() with the appropriate Address at the start of this function

findings.message: Function 'add_liquidity' takes Address parameter 'to' but doesn't authenticate it
findings.suggestion: Use require_auth() with the Address parameter to ensure only that user can call

findings.message: Function 'swap' modifies state without authorization check
findings.suggestion: Add require_auth() with the appropriate Address at the start of this function

findings.message: Function 'swap' takes Address parameter 'to' but doesn't authenticate it
findings.suggestion: Use require_auth() with the Address parameter to ensure only that user can call

findings.message: Public function 'get_reserves' has no authorization check — anybody can call
findings.suggestion: Add require_auth() with the appropriate Address if this function should be restricted

findings.message: Storage key 'v1_reserve_a' written with different value types: '0i128' in '__constructor' and '(reserve_a + amount_a)' in 'add_liquidity'
findings.suggestion: Use the same Rust type for all writes to this key, or rename one of the keys

findings.message: Storage key 'v1_reserve_b' written with different value types: '0i128' in '__constructor' and '(reserve_b + amount_b)' in 'add_liquidity'
